Pickleball Drills for Beginners: Skills Boost!

By Billy Walters • 5/2/2024
pickleball-101

Did you know pickleball is quickly becoming a favorite sport in America? With over 3.3 million people playing, it’s easy to see why. Both young and old folks are drawn to this game that’s full of energy. If you’re new and want to shine on the court, practice is key. And pickleball drills are a great way to do that.

Key Takeaways:

  • Pickleball is a rapidly growing sport in America, attracting millions of players.
  • Beginners can boost their skills and confidence in pickleball through targeted drills.
  • Pickleball drills help players improve their technique, consistency, and overall gameplay.
  • Practicing pickleball drills regularly can lead to noticeable improvement and increased confidence on the court.
  • By incorporating pickleball drills into your training routine, you can build a solid foundation and progress in your pickleball journey.

Why Pickleball Drills Make You Better

Pickleball drills are much more than just working out. They are based on science to boost your game’s skills. By adding these drills to your training, you’ll see skill upgrades, better muscle memory, and game performance.

The Science Behind Pickleball Drills

It’s key to know why these drills work. Doing the same action, like a forehand shot, over and over changes your brain. It makes you better at that task.

A key change is how myelin thickens. Myelin wraps around brain cells, helping them work better. By doing drills with the right technique, you build up these brain paths. This makes your shots and decisions on court quicker and better.

“Pickleball drills enable you to perform actions without conscious effort, making your gameplay feel instinctual and effortless.”

Thicker myelin helps your brain work faster. Then, you do pickleball moves with ease and on instinct. Doing drills over time sharpens your skills and makes the game feel natural.

Benefits of Pickleball Drills

Drills improve more than just muscle memory. They help focus and keep your eye on the ball. With practice, you’ll do shots with more accuracy, even when it’s intense.

Also, drills help spot and fix any playing issues. They let you work on one skill at a time. This helps your overall game and strategy.

Pump Up Your Skills With Drills

To get better at pickleball, you need to practice smart. This means working on hits, serves, and more. Using drills helps you improve each part of your game, making you more skilled, precise, and strategic.

Working on drills helps your body remember the moves you need in games. You might drill serves, lower your third shot, or work on your dinking skills. Each drill helps you reach your goals step by step.

If you’re new and want to get better, we’ve got 18 essential drills for you. These cover everything you need to know. Each drill has clear, easy steps to follow for maximum learning.

Here’s a peek at some drills you’ll find:

  1. Groundstroke Consistency Drill
  2. Dink and Drop Drill
  3. Serve and Volley Drill
  4. Sideways Dinking Drill
  5. Third Shot Drop Drill
  6. Poach and Putaway Drill
  7. Cross-Court Dinking Drill
  8. Defensive Lob Drill
  9. Footwork Agility Drill
  10. Game Simulation Drill

These drills don’t just boost your skill set. They also help you think and play smarter. Regular practice with them helps you gain confidence and build a strong strategy. This pushes your game to new heights.

Next, we’ll detail how to do each drill step by step. Stay tuned!

How to Grip a Pickleball Paddle Correctly

Getting your grip right and standing correctly are big parts of playing pickleball well. When holding the paddle, using the right technique is key. It affects how well you control the ball and how powerful your hits are. Your footwork and where you stand help you move better, cover the court, and hit the ball well.

Start with how hard you hold the paddle. You need to grip it firmly but not too tight. Gripping too tight can slow your wrist and make your hits less flexible. Holding it too loose can lead to weak control and less power. Find a good balance for a strong yet comfortable grip. This allows you to hit with force and also control where the ball goes.

Now, let’s look at the ways you can grip the paddle and where to place your hands. There are three main grips: Eastern, Western, and Continental. Eastern gives you a fine balance of power and control. Western is good for more power and spin but reduces control. Continental is useful when you need to switch quickly between forehand and backhand.

For where to hold the paddle, position it so it goes across your palm at an angle. This helps your wrist work better and make your movements smoother. Keep your fingers relaxed around the handle to avoid being too tense when you hit the ball.

Your feet and how you stand are also very important. Good footwork means you can reach the ball easily and get ready to hit it. Being light on your feet, balanced, and quick to move in any direction is key. Your body’s alignment towards the way you’re hitting and the net helps you hit with more power, stay steady, and be accurate.

To wrap up, having the right grip and stance is vital for playing pickleball well. The way you hold the paddle, your footwork, and body position all matter for a great game. Next, we’ll go over how to hold a pickleball paddle right and offer footwork and stance tips.

10 Pickleball Drills To Leverage Your Game For Beginners

I’ve put together a list of 10 top pickleball drills for beginners. These drills cover things like hitting the ball consistently and practicing tricky defensive moves.

Each drill has clear steps. They help you improve important skills like control and strategy. By doing these drills, beginners can get better at pickleball quickly.

1. Groundstroke Consistency Drill

This drill is all about hitting the ball properly every time.

Instructions:

  1. Stay in the center of your half of the court.
  2. Your partner throws you the ball from the opposite side.
  3. Hit it back with a strong, accurate swing.
  4. Work on keeping a smooth timing and moving your feet well.

2. Dink and Drop Drill

This one teaches you how to do a dink shot and a drop shot well.

Instructions:

  1. Begin close to the kitchen line.
  2. Hit the ball softly with your partner, preventing it from touching the ground.
  3. Switch sides of the net and keep it in control.
  4. This helps with your drop shot, aiming it low over the net.

3. Serve and Volley Drill

This drill improves your serving aim and hitting the ball right at the net.

Instructions:

  1. Start throwing the ball from the back and serve to the front.
  2. Step ahead and hit the ball your partner sends back.
  3. Work on different types of volleys, from soft to fast.
  4. Keep serving and hitting back and forth with your partner.

4. Sideways Dinking Drill

This drill gets you better at dinking while stepping side to side.

Instructions:

  1. Position yourself in the middle of the baseline.
  2. Let your partner serve you from their end.
  3. Move left and right while dinking the ball softly back.
  4. Try to keep it under control and in the right spot.

5. Third Shot Drop Drill

This exercise is all about perfecting the third shot drop.

Instructions:

  1. Begin close to the base on your side.
  2. Your partner serves to you from across the court.
  3. Send back a soft third shot that lands by the net closely.
  4. Then, switch places and take turns serving and dropping the shot.

6. Poach and Putaway Drill

This drill increases your quickness and finishing shots around the net.

Instructions:

  1. Stand close to the net, your partner on the other side.
  2. They toss balls for you to quickly pounce on and hit back.
  3. Practice grabbing and finishing the shots that are yours to take.

7. Cross-Court Dinking Drill

This drill aims to make you accurate and in control when hitting the ball cross-court.

Instructions:

  1. Place yourself not far from the baseline.
  2. Dink across to your partner gently.
  3. Aim to keep it low and close to the far line.
  4. Switch between across and down the line with your partner.

8. Defensive Lob Drill

This practice makes you good at lobbing the ball high and deep.

Instructions:

  1. Stay at the back while your partner intentionally throws the ball high and deep.
  2. Try to send it equally deep but also high over the net in a defensive lob.
  3. Focus on getting a good amount of height and distance.

9. Footwork Agility Drill

This is all about quick and smart moves on the court.

Instructions:

  1. Put cones or markers randomly on your side.
  2. Go fast and touch each one with your foot as quickly as you can.
  3. Work on moving with speed, balance, and coordination.

10. Game Simulation Drill

This drill sets up real game situations, improving your thinking and decision making.

Instructions:

  1. Play a practice game with a partner or someone else.
  2. Try out different strategies and shot choices during the game.
  3. Review the game to see how you can do better next time.
  4. Keep repeating this to make your overall pickleball skills better.

Conclusion

For beginners, learning pickleball means embracing essential drills. These drills focus on key parts of the game, like technique and accuracy. They help players grow and enjoy pickleball more.

Using these drills in regular practice can make a big difference. Players get better and feel more capable on the court. The constant practice turns movements into automatic actions. This means players can react fast and think smart during games.

If you’re new to pickleball, don’t wait to start on these drills. They will push your skills forward. With hard work and sticking to it, you’ll quickly notice improvement. And you’ll begin to love the ways your ability and game plan evolve. So, grab your paddle and dive into this fun, challenging sport!

FAQ

What are pickleball drills?

Pickleball drills are specific exercises to make you better. They work on skills, technique, and how you play the game. They’re especially great for those just starting out to get them playing better.

How do pickleball drills improve skills?

These drills repeat the right moves, making them second nature. This thickens myelin, speeding up how your brain and body work together. So, you get better at the game faster and play more naturally.

What areas of the game can be targeted with pickleball drills?

Drills can focus on serving, dropping shots on the third try, dinking (using soft shots close to the net), and more. Each drill is set to boost specific skills like technique and game accuracy for new players. It helps them get better overall.

How do grip and body positioning impact pickleball shots?

The right grip and where your body is really matter in pickleball. A good paddle grip gives you better power and paddle angle. Your body’s position makes you faster, lets you hit more shots, and covers the court better.

What are some recommended beginner pickleball drills?

For starters, here are 10 top drills to try:
1. Hitting the ball low consistently
2. Practicing gentle shots near the net
3. Serving and quickly moving up to the net
4. Soft shots to the side
5. Dropping the ball carefully after the serve
6. Covering for your partner, then finishing the point
7. Placing your shot across the court
8. Sending the ball high and deep in the opponent’s court
9. Moving your feet quickly
10. Playing a game to practice different skills

How can pickleball drills help beginners improve their game?

Regular practice with pickleball drills helps new players improve quickly. By focusing on different parts of the game, they get better at control and accuracy. This leads to a significant step up in the game, boosting confidence too.
